articles.key_driver: Mortgage rates hitting seven-month highs matter most because they tighten housing-related credit demand and can amplify pressure on bank origination volumes and commercial real estate financing conditions.

The balance of evidence suggests margin and credit-pressure risks are building, but not yet at levels consistent with severe financial distress.
